_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in identifying regional languages and dialects
reference_document_element_description: Skill in fine-tuning pre-trained language models for domain-specific tasks, including dataset preparation, training configuration, and evaluation design.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI AI domain. Skill AI-S-011 is the strongest semantic match.
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.9/10 (raw 0.286). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. NICE-only terms [dialects, identifying, languages, regional] vs WIDAI-only terms [configuration, dataset, design, domain, evaluation, fine] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.872 (P=0.891, R=0.854). P exceeds R by 0.037 — the NICE element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 17 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.8%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in identifying regional languages and dialects" and "Skill in fine-tuning pre-trained language models for domain-specific tasks, incl". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.464 > STS 0.286 (gap +0.178). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.5%. Shared tokens: [skill]. NICE-only: [dialects, identifying, languages, regional]. WIDAI-only: [configuration, dataset, design, domain, evaluation, fine].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.8% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.178 with low STS 0.286. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
